name: A & V Reality Transportation
recent_news: Incorporated Nov 2014, began operating May 2015 in Doral, FL; BBB profile shows C+ rating with one unresolved complaint. Appears to operate as a small non-emergency / special-needs transport provider rather than a Miami-Dade approved school bus contractor (not listed on the M-DCPS approved private bus companies list).
fit_notes: Micro 3-employee Doral operator; ~$196k revenue and likely a single-van NEMT/special-needs service. Sub-scale and not confirmed as a yellow-bus school contractor; very low priority for Summit unless used as a Miami-Dade route bolt-on with consolidation.

name: Seacoast Student Transport LLC
recent_news: No web presence located; Nashua School District student transportation is contracted with First Student (EQT-owned), so Seacoast Student Transport is not the Nashua district contractor. Possibly a small private or charter-school shuttle operator at 159 Main St, Nashua; may be related to or distinct from Seacoast Learning Collaborative (Rochester, NH special-needs transport).
fit_notes: Very small (8 employees, ~$339k revenue) and the main Nashua district is locked up by First Student. Limited tuck-in attractiveness; would only matter as a sub-scale niche acquirer of routes if confirmed to hold private-school or SPED contracts.

name: In School Bus Inc
recent_news: Listed in Chamber of Commerce directory as 'InSchoolbus' in Cambridge, MA 02139, categorized closer to educational consulting than a fleet operator. No evidence of an active yellow-bus fleet; Cambridge Public Schools transportation is handled separately. Could be a planning/route-consulting micro-business rather than a bus contractor.
fit_notes: Likely not a fleet-owning school bus operator. Sub-scale and probably out of scope as a Summit tuck-in; no acquisition rationale absent confirmation of buses and contracts.

name: Mystic School Transportation
districts_or_customers: South Orlando (Hunters Creek, Southport) families/private schools
recent_news: Long-running (since 1994) door-to-door private school transportation business operating in south Orlando and parts of Kissimmee; active Instagram and Yelp presence and US DOT #2825139 on FMCSA SAFER. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Family-owned, ~30+ years in Orlando, but very small (2 employees, ~$143k revenue) and looks more like a van-based private-pay door-to-door service than a yellow-bus district contractor. Low priority as a stand-alone tuck-in; potentially interesting only as a customer-list bolt-on for an Orlando-area platform route.

name: School Bell Transportation Svc
recent_news: No website, phone, or news presence identified. Not listed on the Miami-Dade County Public Schools approved private bus companies roster, suggesting it is not an active M-DCPS-authorized yellow-bus contractor. Likely a micro single-van private-pay school transport in southwest Miami.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (2 employees, ~$131k revenue) Miami micro-operator with no online footprint and no confirmed district contracts. Not a meaningful Summit tuck-in candidate.
